title: DapperPlanning Core Foundation
description:
## Executive Summary
Implementation of the core MVC architecture and native UI for the DapperPlanning tool to provide a high-performance alternative to web-based planning tools.

## Business Value
- **Objective:** Eliminate clunky project management tools by providing a native environment for software planning.
- **Key Results (KPIs):** 100% adherence to defined Agile data hierarchies and reduced context switching.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] **Scenario 1:** The tool provides a native desktop GUI with adjustable and scalable window management.
- [x] **Scenario 2:** Components communicate strictly through a centralized EventDispatcher to ensure decoupling.

## Dependencies
- **Upstream:** Python 3 standard libraries and Tkinter.
- **Downstream:** GitLab API synchronization engine.

## Target Audience
Software Engineers, Product Owners, and Scrum Masters.

## Out of Scope
- [ ] Draft state management (deferred to Phase 4).
- [ ] Environment profiles for multiple GitLab instances (deferred).

## Security & Compliance
- [ ] Does this impact PII/PHI? No.
- [ ] Is a security review required? Yes, for Personal Access Token (PAT) storage.

features.title: Native Desktop GUI & Event System
features.description:
## Feature Description
Primary application window featuring a horizontal paned layout and an event-driven communication system.

## User Impact
Users experience a high-performance desktop environment that reflects real-time updates to the agile hierarchy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] **Scenario 1:** UI elements scale properly in windowed mode to prevent button disappearance.
- [x] **Scenario 2:** Selecting a tree node dispatches a selection event to populate the editor.

## Technical Notes / Implementation Details
- Architecture considerations: Uses ttk for consistent themed widgets across OS platforms.
- API endpoints affected: None (local UI only).

## Out of Scope
- [ ] Interactive live-walkthrough tutorials.

stories.title: Thread-Safe Event Dispatcher
stories.description:
## User Story
**As a** Developer, 
**I want** a centralized event system, 
**So that** I can decouple UI components from data logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] **Scenario 1:** Dispatches from non-main threads are safely routed to the Tkinter main loop.

## Technical Notes / Implementation Details
- Architecture considerations: Uses `threading.get_ident()` to ensure thread safety during dispatch.

## Testing Notes
- Edge cases to consider: Rapid-fire event dispatching during UI heavy loads.

features.title: Data Persistence Layer
features.description:
## Feature Description
A polymorphic adapter system for saving and loading the workspace in JSON and CSV formats.

## User Impact
Users can persist their planning data locally and export it to CSV as a fallback for manual uploads.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] **Scenario 1:** The application can rebuild a full object hierarchy from a flattened CSV file.
- [x] **Scenario 2:** Local state is saved to OS-appropriate data directories.

## Technical Notes / Implementation Details
- Architecture considerations: Uses the Factory pattern to retrieve the correct adapter based on file extension.

stories.title: Hierarchy Flattening Utility
stories.description:
## User Story
**As a** Developer, 
**I want** to convert nested Epic objects into flat dictionary rows, 
**So that** I can support CSV exports.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] **Scenario 1:** All child items (Features and Stories) are correctly mapped to their Parent IDs during flattening.

features.title: GitLab Integration Engine
features.description:
## Feature Description
A dedicated service layer for bidirectional synchronization with GitLab projects and groups.

## User Impact
Users can directly push their planned backlog to GitLab to generate Epics and Issues.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] **Scenario 1:** The GitLabClient successfully creates Epics with specific labels and metadata.
- [x] **Scenario 2:** Users can manage authentication tokens and host URLs through a secure dialog.

## Security & Compliance
- [ ] Is a security review required? Yes, for PAT encryption and storage.

stories.title: Authenticated REST Client
stories.description:
## User Story
**As a** Developer, 
**I want** a secure wrapper for GitLab API requests, 
**So that** I can automate issue creation without external dependencies.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] **Scenario 1:** The client uses `urllib.request` to avoid heavy external libraries.
